verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: All mounts unwound on exit; No dangling namespaces; Host state restored; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red against the pre-delivery surface (engine absent), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.mount_evidence: An envelope with GPU passthrough and its own >10-entry mount table exits; the host /proc/mounts is byte-identical before and after — mounts are namespace-scoped and unwind with the namespace.
gate_result.namespace_evidence: leakcheck scans every /proc/*/environ for the envelope id and the host mount table for residue: leaked_processes=0, host_mount_residue=0, clean=true after exit (also enforced with --die-with-parent).
gate_result.restore_evidence: An --isolate-net envelope run leaves the host interface table (/proc/net/dev) unchanged — acquired resources release with the namespace and the host returns to its prior state.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Host mount-table and interface-table invariance measured around real envelope runs; leakcheck executed live.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-067 is complete: envelope teardown is zero-leak — all envelope mounts unwind with the namespace on exit (host mount table byte-identical), no dangling namespaces or processes survive (leakcheck clean per envelope id), and acquired resources release so host state is restored.
